statement.p: Town A and Town B are 200 miles apart. A boat traveled from Town A to Town B, and then back to Town A. Since the river flows from Town B to Town A, the boats speed was 30 miles per hour faster when it traveled from Town B to Town A. The whole trip took 25 hours. Answer the following questions:
The boat traveled from Town A to Town B at the speed of miles per hour.
The boat traveled from Town B back to Town A at the speed of miles per hour.

solution.p: Assume that the boat traveled from Town A to Town B at x miles per hour.
Then, it traveled from Town B back to Town A at x+30 miles per hour.
Using the formula time=\frac{distance}{speed}, we know the boat traveled from Town A to Town B in \frac{200}{x} hours; and it traveled back from Town B to Town A in \frac{200}{x+30} hours.
Its given that the whole trip took 25 hours, so we can write an equation and solve it:
When numbers are big, we use the quadratic formula to solve the quadratic equation:
In this situation, x=-24 is not a valid solution, since speed cannot be negative, x=10 is the only solution. The boat traveled from Town A to Town B at the speed of {10} miles per hour.
The boat traveled from Town B back to Town A at the speed of {10}+30={40} miles per hour.
